name: Amsterdam
intro: Amsterdam is one of Europe's most dog-friendly cities, with parks, canals, and welcoming hotels.

name: Paris
intro: Paris welcomes pets in many hotels, cafés, and even some restaurants.

name: Biarritz
intro: Biarritz is a paradise for dogs and their owners, with beach access and pet-friendly hotels.

name: Barcelona
intro: Barcelona has a growing pet-friendly scene with many hotels welcoming dogs of all sizes.

name: Berlin
intro: Berlin is one of the most dog-friendly capitals in Europe, with extensive parks and pet-welcoming accommodation.

name: Lisbon
intro: Lisbon's mild climate and relaxed culture make it ideal for travelling with your pet.

name: Rome
intro: Rome combines ancient grandeur with a surprisingly relaxed attitude toward pets — dogs are welcome in many piazzas, parks, and hotel terraces.

name: Madrid
intro: Madrid is one of Europe's most dog-friendly capitals, with sprawling parks, dog-welcoming terraces, and a thriving pet culture.

name: Prague
intro: Prague's compact old town and riverside parks make it an underrated gem for travelling with dogs.

name: Vienna
intro: Vienna's grand parks, dog-friendly cafés, and highly-rated hotels make it a top destination for pet owners in Central Europe.

name: Copenhagen
intro: Copenhagen leads Europe in sustainability and pet-friendliness — dogs are welcomed in shops, restaurants, and on public transport.

name: Stockholm
intro: Stockholm's archipelago setting, vast green spaces, and dog-welcoming culture make it one of Scandinavia's best cities for pet travel.

name: Munich
intro: Munich's English Garden, beer garden culture (dogs welcome), and excellent hotels make it ideal for a city break with your pet.

name: Zurich
intro: Zurich has some of Europe's highest standards for pet welfare — dogs ride trams, enter shops, and are welcomed in many top hotels.

name: Nice
intro: Nice's Mediterranean climate, Promenade des Anglais, and abundance of terrace restaurants make it a superb destination for pets year-round.

name: Bordeaux
intro: Bordeaux pairs world-class wine country with a relaxed, dog-friendly urban culture and excellent boutique hotels.

name: Lyon
intro: Lyon's gastronomic culture extends to its terrace restaurants and bouchons, many of which welcome well-behaved dogs.

name: Bruges
intro: Bruges is one of Belgium's most charming cities for a pet-friendly break — canals, quiet cobblestone streets, and welcoming hotels.

name: Budapest
intro: Budapest's riverside parks, thermal bath culture, and affordable luxury hotels make it a rising star for pet-friendly travel in Eastern Europe.

name: Dubrovnik
intro: Dubrovnik's dramatic coastline, island day-trips, and boutique hotels outside the Old Town walls offer a memorable setting for pet owners.

name: Porto
intro: Porto's hilly streets, river esplanades, and laid-back attitude make it one of Portugal's most welcoming cities for travelling with a dog.

name: Seville
intro: Seville's orange-tree lined streets, riverside promenades, and vibrant terrace culture make it one of Andalusia's most dog-friendly cities.

name: Valencia
intro: Valencia's sunny climate, Turia river park, and dog-welcoming beaches make it a superb year-round destination for pet travel.

name: Malaga
intro: Malaga combines a sunny Mediterranean climate with a relaxed attitude toward dogs — ideal for a beach-and-city break with your pet.

name: Florence
intro: Florence's Renaissance beauty is matched by a surprisingly welcoming attitude toward dogs — in restaurants, on the Arno riverbanks, and in the Boboli gardens.

name: Venice
intro: Venice is surprisingly dog-friendly — dogs travel on the vaporetto, explore quiet campi, and are welcomed in many trattorias.

name: Ghent
intro: Ghent is one of Belgium's most progressive cities — a strong cycling and pet culture, compact centre, and excellent hotels make it ideal for dog owners.

name: Antwerp
intro: Antwerp's fashion-forward, dog-welcoming culture, riverside parks, and boutique hotel scene make it Belgium's most exciting city for pet travel.

name: Edinburgh
intro: Edinburgh's dramatic hills, Arthur's Seat, and deeply dog-welcoming pub culture make it one of Britain's best cities for a break with your dog.

name: Dublin
intro: Dublin's pub gardens, Phoenix Park, and warm Irish welcome extend to dogs — making it one of the most dog-friendly capitals in Europe.

name: Reykjavik
intro: Reykjavik's clean air, dramatic landscapes, and dog-welcoming culture make it a unique and increasingly popular destination for adventurous pet owners.

name: Ljubljana
intro: Ljubljana — Europe's green capital — is one of the continent's most walkable, bike-friendly, and dog-welcoming small cities.

name: Riga
intro: Riga's Art Nouveau architecture, riverside parks, and affordable hotels make it an emerging gem for pet-friendly city breaks in the Baltics.

name: Tallinn
intro: Tallinn's beautifully preserved medieval old town, coastal paths, and dog-friendly hotels make it a hidden gem for pet travel in Northern Europe.
